title: The number of people at high risk from climate-related hazards varies by region
subtitle: Share of population at high risk from climate-related hazards in 2024
source: World Bank Scorecard Indicator, Hill et al.
visdescription: Map showing the share of regional populations defined as being at high risk from climate-related hazards in 2024.
textParagraph: On this map, the size of the grey circles is proportional to the size of the population of the seven World Bank regions. The blue circles show the size of the population exposed to climate-related hazards. The red circle shows the size of the population at high risk (both exposed to climate-related hazards and in vulnerable conditions). In North America less than 1 percent of the population (approximately 0.6 million people) are at high risk. In contrast, more than a third of the people living in the Middle East, North Africa, Afghanistan and Pakistan and Sub-Saharan Africa regions are at high risk.
